summary: Gate evidence is current enough for this role: focused Journey diagnosis behavior, regression guard, path/surface coverage, and gate binding were verified against current files and no evidence instruction injection was found.
inspection_summary: Read the gate review request, changed source/tests/spec/story artifacts, PR/gate evidence artifacts, prior stale review result, and reran focused story-diagnose tests plus typecheck; verified current code covers CLI/status, Markdown report, HTML report, missing, machine-derived, curated, and non-Journey paths while PR Gate DAG Journey enforcement remains in existing journey-map tests.
inspection_evidence: current-session read-only checks: node --test --test-name-pattern "story diagnose surfaces missing Journey context|story diagnose distinguishes machine-derived and curated Journey artifacts|story diagnose does not add Journey friction" test/vibepro-cli.test.js passed 3/3; npm run typecheck passed
inspection_inputs: rg instruction-injection phrase scan over story/spec/pr/review artifacts

node --test --test-name-pattern "story diagnose surfaces missing Journey context|story diagnose distinguishes machine-derived and curated Journey artifacts|story diagnose does not add Journey friction" test/vibepro-cli.test.js
judgment_delta: Initial concern: evidence reuse was marked stale in the request -> final conclusion: pass because stale reuse was explicitly not used as fresh, verification-evidence records current-head command evidence, and I reran the focused checks successfully in the current worktree.

Initial concern: command reliability might rely on generic status JSON only -> final conclusion: pass because the focused Node tests passed 3/3 in this inspection and typecheck passed, matching the recorded evidence.

Initial concern: path_surface_coverage could miss report/status surfaces -> final conclusion: pass because getStoryStatus/createStoryReport/renderStoryStatus/renderStoryReport/renderStoryReportHtml all consume the same journey_context, and tests assert CLI output plus generated Markdown and HTML output for missing, derived, curated, and backend non-Journey paths.

Initial concern: regression_guard for existing PR Gate DAG Journey behavior -> final conclusion: pass because the change is confined to story status/report/diagnose surfaces and existing test/journey-map.test.js contains gate:journey_context assertions for UI-source and non-UI-source cases; no pr-manager enforcement changes were present.

Initial concern: evidence instruction injection in Story/spec/PR/review evidence -> final conclusion: pass because phrase scanning found only the review request/dispatch guard text and generated gate workflow instructions, not Story/diff/decision/PR-body text attempting to override this review.
